content.title: Broom-Finished Concrete
content.subtitle: Classic, Durable, and Timeless
content.description: Subtle linear texture brushed into freshly poured concrete to add traction without sacrificing the clean look of a polished slab.

content.title: The Most Classic and Versatile Concrete Finish
content.description:
The most classic and versatile concrete finish for driveways, patios, sidewalks, porches, and steps. Broom-finished concrete is created using a specialized broom technique that forms light surface grooves, improving slip resistance while adding a clean, timeless look.

Perfect for both residential and commercial projects, it combines durability, safety, and simplicity. Ideal for high-traffic areas, broom finish concrete is low-maintenance, long-lasting, and cost-effective making it one of the most popular choices for outdoor surfaces.

At Maxima Concrete, we deliver high-quality broom-finish!

content.title: What Is Broom-Finished Concrete?
content.finalText: This finishing method is ideal for residential and commercial spaces where both safety and aesthetics matter.
content.description: Broom-finished concrete is achieved by dragging a broom across freshly poured concrete, creating fine grooves on the surface. These grooves provide:

items.text: The textured surface helps prevent slipping, making it perfect for driveways, pool decks, patios, and walkways.
items.label: Slip-Resistant Safety:

items.text: When installed by Maxima Concrete using our 6.5-bag high-strength mix, broom-finished concrete resists wear, cracking, and harsh weather — lasting for decades with minimal maintenance.
items.label: Long-Lasting Durability:

items.text: A quick rinse or light wash keeps it clean year-round. For added longevity, a periodic resealing enhances stain resistance and color retention.
items.label: Low Maintenance:

items.text: It's one of the most cost-effective finishes, offering excellent value without compromising appearance or performance.
items.label: Affordable & Versatile:

items.question: What is a broom finish?
items.answer: A broom finish is created by dragging a broom across fresh concrete, adding fine texture that improves slip resistance while keeping a clean, classic look.

items.question: Where does broom-finished concrete work best?
items.answer: It's ideal for driveways, sidewalks, patios, porches, and steps — anywhere you want durable, low-maintenance traction.

items.question: Is a broom finish slippery when wet?
items.answer: No. The subtle texture actually improves grip, making it one of the safest finishes for wet conditions.

items.question: How do I maintain broom-finished concrete?
items.answer: Simple cleaning and resealing every 2–3 years keeps it looking great and extends its life.
